criteria.description: Failures are reported only when the signature appears in a genuine output channel or a distinctive diagnostic signal — never solely in narrative, code-edit, or file-read content.
criteria.evidence: TestIssue4FourWayReproThroughEvent: assistant text / Edit newString / codex reasoning carrying 'AssertionError' do NOT classify; only toolUseResult.stderr classifies as test_failure. Corpus sweep (17 missions): total failures 781->422 (-46%) narrative/edit FP reduction.
criteria.verified_by: kent (maintainer) via Claude orchestration + adversarial Codex review
criteria.notes: Per-pattern channel scoping (WP02) + cached-channel event wiring (WP03).

criteria.description: Conditions that legitimately appear in narrative — specifically branch/worktree confusion — continue to be detected with no loss vs. current behavior.
criteria.evidence: TestContractCThroughEvent + TestFingerprintBranchWorktreeDiagnosticScope: distinctive branch_worktree_confusion signature in narrative still classifies (diagnostic scope). Corpus finalize-inbox: the 2 genuine narrative topology detections (#1716/#2046) are preserved (10->2; the 8 dropped were verified FPs: edits/tool-inputs/TodoWrite attachments).
criteria.verified_by: kent (maintainer) via Claude orchestration + channel decomposition
criteria.notes: Distinctiveness gate: only branch_worktree_confusion distinctive patterns are diagnostic-scoped.

criteria.description: Genuine failures present in command/tool output continue to be reported (no loss of true positives across the validation corpus).
criteria.evidence: Corpus SC-003 verified: no event that was a true failure before the change becomes unreported after. review_rejected 37->6 on credential-liveness decomposed — all drops are narrative/command FPs; real structured rejection artifacts survive via tool_result->output. Output-channel failures (CONFLICT, stderr) still classify (TestContractCThroughEvent).
criteria.verified_by: kent (maintainer) via Claude orchestration + corpus decomposition
criteria.notes: SC-003 holds across the corpus sweep.

criteria.description: Plain-text artifact/spec content that only discusses errors is not classified as a failure.
criteria.evidence: TestObjNilChannelRouting (artifact/spec line discussing an error -> not a failure; transcript stray line -> output-eligible) + TestArtifactSuppressionAllFourKinds (work_package/mission_artifact/mission_meta/mission_status_snapshot failures suppressed before aggregation) + §3a exclusion tests (file-read/code-edit content never reaches a channel).

criteria.description: Output that genuinely contains a failure signature is still reported even if it also echoes/quotes failure text.
criteria.evidence: TestIssue4FourWayReproThroughEvent: toolUseResult.stderr = 'AssertionError: boom' classifies as test_failure (genuine output). Output-channel content is always scanned regardless of quoted text (design §8 residual is intentional and correct).
criteria.notes: Output channel is authoritative; quoting is correctly reported.

criteria.description: Failure reporting remains deterministic: identical input logs yield identical classifications.
criteria.evidence: TestChannelDeterminism (sorted map keys, fixed fragment order; 5x loop) + explicit per-pattern scope with a build-time test (TestFingerprintRulesAllScoped) that fails if any pattern is unscoped (no implicit default). Single deterministic extraction pass per event.
criteria.notes: Determinism asserted at the channel layer and enforced by the scope build-gate.
